Title: Elucidating the enzyme network driving Amaryllidaceae alkaloids biosynthesis in Leucojum aestivum

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses tyramine and 3,4-dihydroxybenzaldehyde to form norcraugsodine, which in the reconstructed pathway is further reduced to norbelladine with LaNRII
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmaceutically potent antiviral and anticancer alkaloid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as potentially rate-limiting for norbelladine formation; authors note low quantities suggest this step may be rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with LaNRII enables norbelladine production from tyramine and 3,4-DHBA in planta and in vitro, supporting entry into AA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with substrate feeding; in vitro assay with crude extracts and purified MBP-tagged protein plus LaNRII; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; docking; nanoDSF thermal unfolding
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration; substrate feeding with tyramine and 3,4-DHBA
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from Leucojum aestivum transcriptome PRJNA720900
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NBS alone is insufficiently emphasized; efficient norbelladine formation required co-expression with LaNRII due to instability of norcraugsodine. Docking implicated Tyr59, Glu71 and Lys83 in catalysis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LaNBS is a PR10/Bet v1-like enzyme candidate for the first condensation step of AA biosynthesis. 2. Co-expression with LaNRII and precursor feeding produced norbelladine in N. benthamiana and in vitro. 3. The low yield suggests the norbelladine-forming step may be a bottleneck for pathway engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful entry-step enzyme for reconstructing AA biosynthesis in heterologous hosts; likely should be stacked with LaNRII and downstream enzymes to improve flux toward medicinal alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low product yield suggests limited flux if used alone.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Foundation steps and all three phenol-coupled branches
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Short-chain dehydrogenase/reductase (SDR) family, tropinone reductase subfamily
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces norcraugsodine to norbelladine and also reduces multiple phenol-coupled intermediates including noroxopluviine to norpluviine, N-demethylnarwedine to norgalanthamine, and noroxomaritidine to normaritidine and/or oxomaritinamine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmaceutically potent antiviral and anticancer alkaloid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms branch intermediates and reduced products leading to terminal alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream and midstream branch-processing reduction steps
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting; established as broad-acting multifunctional reductase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes norbelladine biosynthesis and reduces products from all three phenol-coupling branches, greatly expanding AA product formation in planta
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with substrate feeding; co-expression with LaCYP96T1/2; in vitro assays with purified protein and microsomes; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; docking; nanoDSF thermal unfolding
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration; feeding with tyramine and 3,4-DHBA or 4'-O-methylnorbelladine; NADPH-dependent assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from Leucojum aestivum transcriptome PRJNA720900
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Displays strong substrate promiscuity; catalytic triad predicted as Ser159/Tyr173/Lys177. Authors highlight novelty of reducing all three phenol-coupled product classes.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LaNRII reduces norcraugsodine to norbelladine in the entry pathway. 2. It also reduces intermediates from the o-p', p-o' and p-p' branches, generating norpluviine, norgalanthamine, normaritidine and oxomaritinamine. 3. LaNRII is therefore a multifunctional, promiscuous SDR that broadens AA scaffold production.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High value for synthetic biology because one reductase can service multiple AA branches, simplifying pathway reconstruction and alkaloid diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuity may create mixed-product profiles and reduce selectivity toward a single desired alkaloid.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I O-methyltransferase; SAM-dependent Rossmann-fold methyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group to the 4'-hydroxyl of norbelladine to form 4'-O-methylnorbelladine; can also methylate 3,4-DHBA and under Ni2+ produce minor dimethylnorbelladine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmaceutically potent antiviral and anticancer alkaloid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor / branch-point substrate for CYP96T-catalyzed phenol coupling
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branching precursor-forming step; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces 4'-O-methylnorbelladine in planta and in vitro, enabling downstream formation of multiple AA skeletons
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with norbelladine feeding; co-expression with LaNBS and LaNRII; in vitro assays with purified enzyme and metal-ion tests; LC-MS/MS; localization assay; docking
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration; feeding with norbelladine; Mg2+ versus Ni2+ affects regioselectivity/product profile
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from Leucojum aestivum transcriptome PRJNA720900
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: With Mg2+, 4'-O-methylnorbelladine is the sole major product; with Ni2+, minor 3',4'-O-dimethylnorbelladine appears. Also methylates 3,4-DHBA to vanillin and isovanillin, showing substrate promiscuity.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LaN4'OMT is a cytosolic class I OMT that regiospecifically produces 4'-O-methylnorbelladine in planta. 2. This product serves as the major substrate for CYP96T-dependent phenol coupling. 3. The enzyme shows metal- and substrate-dependent promiscuity, but strongly favors the 4'-O-methylated product under Mg2+ conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential enzyme for supplying the branch-point precursor 4'-O-methylnorbelladine in heterologous AA production systems; metal conditions may be tuned to adjust product profile.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuity under alternative metal conditions may generate by-products and reduce pathway specificity.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Phenol-coupling branch point generating lycorine-, galanthamine- and crinine-type scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative regioselective C-C phenol coupling of 4'-O-methylnorbelladine to generate intermediates corresponding to o-p', p-o', and p-p' coupling products, including noroxopluviine, N-demethylnarwedine, and noroxomaritidine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmaceutically potent antiviral and anticancer alkaloid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Diverts flux toward multiple terminal alkaloid scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-switch enzyme; decisive control point for AA scaffold diversification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables all three phenol-coupling reaction types from 4'-O-methylnorbelladine, with strong production of lycorine-pathway products and detection of crinine/haemanthamine-related products in full pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with 4'-O-methylnorbelladine feeding; co-expression with LaNRII and upstream enzymes; microsomal in vitro assay; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; docking
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration; feeding with 4'-O-methylnorbelladine or upstream precursors; microsomal NADPH-dependent assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from Leucojum aestivum transcriptome PRJNA720900
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the first monocot enzymes reported here to catalyze all three regioselective C-C phenol couplings. In full pathway reconstitution, LaCYP96T1 was more efficient than LaCYP96T2 for lycorine and uniquely supported detectable vittatine/crinine and haemanthamine.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LaCYP96T1 is an ER-localized CYP96T family enzyme acting at the key phenol-coupling branch point. 2. It generates products from all three coupling modes from 4'-O-methylnorbelladine. 3. In the full four-enzyme system, it favored lycorine accumulation and additionally supported crinine/haemanthamine-related product formation more strongly than LaCYP96T2.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value branch-switch enzyme for engineering medicinal AA diversity, especially lycorine and crinine/haemanthamine-related scaffolds.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuity may complicate selective production of a single alkaloid; activity in yeast was unsuccessful in this study, indicating host-dependence.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Phenol-coupling branch point generating lycorine-, galanthamine- and crinine-type scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative regioselective C-C phenol coupling of 4'-O-methylnorbelladine to generate intermediates corresponding to o-p', p-o', and p-p' coupling products, including noroxopluviine, N-demethylnarwedine, and noroxomaritidine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmaceutically potent antiviral and anticancer alkaloid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Diverts flux toward multiple terminal alkaloid scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-switch enzyme; decisive control point for AA scaffold diversification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables all three phenol-coupling reaction types from 4'-O-methylnorbelladine and in the full pathway favors lycorine and norgalanthamine formation, though generally less efficiently than LaCYP96T1 for lycorine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with 4'-O-methylnorbelladine feeding; co-expression with LaNRII and upstream enzymes; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; docking; localization assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration; feeding with 4'-O-methylnorbelladine or upstream precursors
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from Leucojum aestivum transcriptome PRJNA720900
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported here alongside LaCYP96T1 as a monocot CYP capable of all three regioselective C-C phenol couplings. Lycorine was directly detected upon LaCYP96T2 expression with 4'-O-methylnorbelladine, and full pathway assays suggested relatively better norgalanthamine formation than LaCYP96T1.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LaCYP96T2 is an ER-localized CYP96T enzyme that accepts 4'-O-methylnorbelladine and supports o-p', p-o' and p-p' coupling outcomes. 2. It contributed to lycorine production and supported norgalanthamine formation in the reconstructed pathway. 3. Its product profile differs quantitatively from LaCYP96T1, indicating exploitable isoform bias.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful alternative CYP96T isoform for tuning AA branch output, especially when aiming for lycorine/norgalanthamine-type products.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuity produces mixed scaffolds; lower lycorine productivity than LaCYP96T1 in the four-enzyme system.
